Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for the AWS cost spike escalation and root cause analysis using search_emails (query: "AWS" or "cost spike" or "root cause" or "$42,870"). Find Marcus Thorne's escalation email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com (CC: elena.rostova@moveops.com) to Samira flagging the March AWS bill at $42,870, roughly three times the typical monthly baseline of about $14,300, with a breakdown showing EC2 Compute at $28,450, S3 Storage and Transfer at $5,500, and RDS Database at $8,920. Find Samira's acknowledgment email from samira.tariq@moveops.com (CC: elena.rostova@moveops.com) confirming she is prioritizing this as a P1 issue, setting up a dedicated Slack channel (#root-cause-aws-spike), and bringing Lena on for AWS Cost Explorer and CloudWatch analysis. Find Samira's formal root cause analysis email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to Marcus and Elena (CC: julian.brooks@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com) detailing the full root cause: Vantage Distributed enterprise onboarding started March 21 with over 1,200 concurrent users (1,047 employees and about 180 contractors), self-service portal batch imports caused sustained high API traffic for over seven days, EC2 autoscaling group increased from 4 to 18 instances, 47,000 document uploads to S3, and RDS connection spike. The email includes recommendations for cost alerting thresholds and pricing model updates. Find Elena Rostova's directive reply (CC: julian.brooks, catalina.dubois, lena.bjorkstrom, alejandro.fuentes) confirming the Vantage deal is still a win but requiring: cost alert thresholds implemented this week, the infrastructure cost estimator tool prioritized, a pricing model review meeting arranged for the following week, and any enterprise deal exceeding 500 employees to require engineering cost sign-off before confirming pricing.
Search Slack channel #root-cause-aws-spike (C009) for the full investigation thread using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "AWS" or "cost" or "root cause", filter_in_channel: "C009") or conversations_history (channel_id: "C009"). Find Samira's investigation kickoff listing the March bill at $42,870 vs the baseline of approximately $14,300 and the investigation plan covering AWS Cost Explorer by service and date, CloudWatch metrics for compute and storage, deployment logs from March 20 onward, and customer onboarding event timelines. Find Samira's first update identifying costs as steady at around $475 per day until 3/20, a slight increase on 3/21, significant EC2 and S3 cost rises from 3/22 to 3/23, and a sharp surge to over $1,850 per day by 3/28. Find Lena running billing breakdown queries from Cost Explorer, CloudWatch, and API gateway logs. Find Samira's recap identifying autoscaling from 4 to 18 instances, 47K document uploads versus the usual 2K per month, and the spike start date of 3/21 matching a major onboarding event. Find Marcus asking the customer team to confirm whether a large enterprise onboarding happened around 3/21. Find Julian's thread reply confirming the Vantage Distributed onboarding of 1,200+ users started on 3/21. Find Samira's confirmed root cause summary stating this was legitimate traffic from a paying client, autoscaling functioned as intended, and the problem was that no one accounted for the infrastructure cost of onboarding 1,200 users at once. Find Marcus's response calling it a process failure and requesting infrastructure cost estimates before enterprise pricing is confirmed. Find Samira's post confirming she emailed Marcus and Elena the formal root cause analysis with a complete breakdown and recommendations. Find the resolution summary post listing the current status: root cause identified and verified, no infrastructure misconfiguration, root cause report distributed, CEO directives issued, AWS cost alert thresholds in progress with an ETA of Wednesday, cost tagging per customer in progress with an ETA of Wednesday, and pricing model review meeting scheduled for the following week.
Search Slack channel #engineering (C003) for the remediation task list and Lena's CloudWatch progress using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "cost alert" or "CloudWatch" or "budget tag" or "engineering" or "remediation", filter_in_channel: "C003") or conversations_history (channel_id: "C003"). Find Samira's initial warning message about daily compute expenses surging from around $475 to more than $1,850, with storage costs also rising. Find Lena's reply noting the API gateway is recording roughly ten times the typical daily request volume starting around March 21 and offering to investigate CloudWatch dashboards. Find Samira's engineering remediation task list organized by priority: P0 for that week including setting AWS cost alert thresholds with notifications at 50%, 100%, and 200% of the monthly baseline through CloudWatch plus Slack integration to #finance, and implementing budget tags for all key AWS resources to allocate costs by customer; P1 for the next two weeks including developing an infrastructure cost estimator tool for sales and improving the slow relocation matching algorithm queries Lena discovered; P2 for the quarter including reviewing the autoscaling policy for spot instances and creating a monthly cost-per-customer report for finance. Find Lena's response confirming she has begun setting up CloudWatch alarms using a Lambda function that checks Cost Explorer daily and notifies #finance if thresholds are exceeded, budget tags will be added through a Terraform update tagged by customer ID so finance can track costs per customer, and she found three slow queries in the relocation matching algorithm that are not utilizing indexes correctly. These messages establish the specific controls that were supposed to be implemented, which the prompt asks the agent to report on.
Search Slack channel #finance (C005) for Alejandro's cost entry and Marcus's escalation using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "AWS" or "$42,870" or "March" or "cloud", filter_in_channel: "C005") or conversations_history (channel_id: "C005"). Find Alejandro Fuentes's message reporting he entered the March AWS invoice into QuickBooks at $42,870 versus the typical monthly baseline of around $14,300, with EC2 compute alone at $28,450. Find Marcus Thorne's reply in the same thread requesting Alejandro compile a month-over-month comparison of cloud expenses for the past six months and forwarding an escalation email to Samira. Find Marcus's pricing model review post listing the agenda: cost model per employee for infrastructure, threshold for enterprise deals where Elena requested any deals exceeding 500 employees require engineering cost approval, margin evaluation on the Vantage Distributed deal with Alejandro crunching the numbers given the AWS overage, and continuous cost monitoring dashboards. This provides financial context for the cost monitoring dashboard scoping and reveals the pricing model review work happening in parallel.
Search QuickBooks for the AWS March bill using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching "AWS" or "Amazon" or "March 2026"). Find bill-aws-march-001 from vendor Amazon Web Services (vendor-aws-001) totaling $42,870 with line items: EC2 Compute at $28,450, S3 Storage and Transfer at $5,500, and RDS Database at $8,920. This confirms the actual financial impact in QuickBooks and provides source data for the dashboard's historical trend views component.
Search Linear for existing tickets related to the AWS cost spike or infrastructure monitoring remediation using linear_list_issues (query: "AWS" or "cost alert" or "CloudWatch" or "infrastructure cost" or "budget tag" or "autoscaling" or "cost monitoring"). Discover that no dedicated Linear tickets exist for the AWS remediation items from Samira's task list (cost alert thresholds, budget tags, infrastructure cost estimator, slow query fixes, autoscaling review, monthly cost report). The remediation work was assigned and tracked informally through Slack posts in C009 and C003 but was never formalized into Linear issues. This is a significant finding for the scoping summary: the prompt asks to link the new dashboard issues to existing remediation tickets and to comment on the RCA tracking ticket, but neither exists in Linear as a formal issue. The agent should note this tracking gap.
Search Linear for ExpenseBot project tickets and Anh's engineering work using linear_list_issues (query: "expense" or "dashboard" or "type validation" or project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer") or linear_list_issues (assignee: "moveops_anh_nguyen"). Find Anh's assigned tickets: issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208, Build rule configuration UI for per-client policy management, state: todo, priority P2, assigned to Anh), issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218, Dashboard: Add type validation to policy config editor, state: in_progress, priority P2, assigned to Anh), and issue_bulk_export (ENG-213, Dashboard: Bulk export and policy version history, state: backlog, priority P3, assigned to Anh). Also find related project tickets that touch data integrity: issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214, fix Vectral config, in_progress, assigned to Lena), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215, fix Mosaic config with malformed JSON types, in_progress, assigned to Lena), and issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217, harden policy ingestion pipeline to remove silent fallback and add type enforcement, in_progress, assigned to Lena). These tickets establish what data integrity and pipeline reliability work already exists on the engineering board and whether any of it touches the cost data pipeline the dashboard would rely on.
Search Linear for Dmitri's schema validation and audit work using linear_list_issues (query: "schema" or "validation" or "duplicate" or "audit" or assignee: "moveops_dmitri_volkov"). Find issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216, full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses, state: in_progress, priority: Urgent, assigned to Dmitri), issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212, policy config validation and malformed data handling, state: in_progress, priority P2, assigned to Dmitri), and issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220, stipend duplicate detection using receipt hash fingerprinting and PDF metadata similarity scoring, state: todo, priority P1, assigned to Dmitri). Read comments on these tickets using linear_list_comments. On ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) find comment_dmitri_audit_results documenting the completed audit of 47 expenses: 39 accurate, 5 incorrectly rejected at Vectral, 3 mistakenly approved at Mosaic, resulting in 83% accuracy and $885 total financial exposure. On ENG-212 find comment_dmitri_validation_tests listing six implemented schema validation tests (type assertions, required fields, config-vs-source comparison, exclusion list enum validation, positive limit values, and no silent fallback), all passing with corrected configs but failing with the original production configs. On ENG-220 find comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context noting Dmitri will begin Layer 1 hash dedup implementation and will work with Lena on ingestion pipeline changes, with Lena confirming S3 hash data is already accessible. These tickets and comments show the current state of data pipeline reliability work and whether any of it could affect cost data ingestion for the dashboard.
Search Slack #engineering (C003) for discussions about data validation and pipeline reliability using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "type validation" or "schema validation" or "data validation" or "pipeline" or "ingestion", filter_in_channel: "C003"). Find Anh's message proposing to add type-checking in the dashboard's policy config editor to prevent non-numeric limits and non-array exclusion lists from being saved as JSON, described as a belt-and-suspenders approach, with thumbsup reactions from both Samira and Lena. Find Dmitri's reply agreeing and listing schema validation priorities: type assertions for all config fields, required field checks, generated configs compared against source spreadsheet values, exclusion list entries validated against category enums, and recommending a full audit of all 47 auto-processed expenses. Find Samira's message taking responsibility for the silent fallback in the ingestion pipeline, asking Dmitri to prioritize validation tests and Lena to fix both client configs immediately. Find Lena's earlier message about having implemented basic schema validation in the policy config loader ensuring it rejects JSON missing essential fields, but noting that the effects of subtly malformed data like exclusion lists with wrong types or limits stored as strings instead of numbers had not been tested. These threads establish the data validation concerns and pipeline reliability issues that may be relevant to the cost monitoring dashboard's data sources.
Look up the #engineering Slack channel ID using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel"). Confirm that channel C003 corresponds to #engineering for posting the technical scoping summary. Also note C009 for #root-cause-aws-spike and C005 for #finance from the investigation phase.
Look up the Engineering team in Linear using linear_list_teams or linear_get_team (query: "Engineering"). Find team_engineering with key "ENG" which is needed for creating the new cost monitoring project and issues.
Create a new project in Linear for the cost monitoring dashboard work using linear_create_project (name: "Cost Monitoring Dashboard" or similar, team: "team_engineering", description covering the goal of giving engineering and finance real-time visibility into infrastructure resource consumption to prevent future cost surprises like the March 2026 AWS spike triggered by the Vantage Distributed onboarding).
Create four separate Linear issues under the new cost monitoring project using linear_create_issue for each, all with team: "team_engineering" and project referencing the newly created project ID. Issue 1: Real-time resource consumption tracking, covering a live dashboard view of current AWS compute, storage, and database usage with per-service breakdown. Issue 2: Threshold-based alerting, covering configurable notifications at defined percentages of the monthly baseline, connecting to the P0 CloudWatch plus Slack integration that Lena started but may not have completed. Issue 3: Per-client cost attribution, covering the ability to break down infrastructure costs by customer using the budget tagging approach Lena began implementing via Terraform. Issue 4: Historical trend views, covering time-series visualization of monthly costs for spotting patterns, with the March 2026 spike as the reference event showing costs jumping from approximately $14,300 baseline to $42,870.
Attempt to link the new issues to existing remediation tickets from the AWS incident using linear_update_issue to add related issue references or update descriptions. Since no dedicated AWS remediation Linear tickets exist (discovered in OE 6), use linear_update_issue to embed references to the Slack-based remediation tracking (C009 resolution summary, C003 task list) in the new issue descriptions, and note that remediation items were never formalized into Linear issues. If the agent finds any existing engineering tickets tangentially related to infrastructure monitoring, those could be linked. The absence of formal tracking tickets is itself a finding that should be noted in the scoping summary.
Check the status of the remediation controls that were supposed to be implemented after the AWS spike and flag any that look stale. The agent should cross-reference Samira's P0/P1/P2 task list from C003 (CloudWatch cost alerts, budget tags, infrastructure cost estimator, slow query fixes, autoscaling review, monthly cost report) against what was actually completed. Lena said she would have cost alerts and budget tags ready by Wednesday (around April 2) but there is no follow-up confirmation in Slack or Linear that these were completed. The slow RDS queries were described as a quick fix but no completion was posted. The infrastructure cost estimator and autoscaling review were lower priority and likely not started. Add comments on any relevant tickets using linear_create_comment noting which controls appear incomplete or unverified. If specific ExpenseBot tickets like ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) or ENG-218 (dashboard type validation) show stale statuses that do not reflect recent work discussed in Slack, comment on those as well.
Search for what the prompt refers to as "the original RCA tracking ticket" using linear_list_issues (query: "RCA" or "root cause" or "AWS cost" or "cost spike" or "March AWS"). Since no formal RCA tracking ticket exists in Linear for the AWS cost incident, the agent should note this gap. The root cause investigation was conducted entirely through the #root-cause-aws-spike Slack channel (C009) and email correspondence, with a resolution summary posted in C009 but never converted to a Linear issue. The agent should add a comment using linear_create_comment on the most relevant existing ticket, or if no suitable ticket exists, include the current status of the remediation controls in the scoping summary posted to #engineering. The status update should cover: root cause identified (Vantage Distributed onboarding), no misconfiguration, RCA distributed to leadership, CEO directives issued, CloudWatch cost alerts started by Lena with no confirmed completion, budget tags started with no confirmed completion, pricing model review meeting referenced by Marcus but outcome not tracked in engineering channels, infrastructure cost estimator not yet started, slow query fixes described as quick but not confirmed, and autoscaling review not yet started.
Post the technical scoping summary in the #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: summary content). The summary should cover: what was found during the AWS cost spike investigation (root cause was Vantage Distributed onboarding of 1,200+ users starting March 21, autoscaling from 4 to 18 EC2 instances, 47K document uploads, $42,870 March bill versus $14,300 baseline), the current status of each remediation control from Samira's task list with specific notes on which appear incomplete or unverified, the finding that AWS remediation was tracked only through Slack posts with no Linear tickets, the new Cost Monitoring Dashboard project and its four component issues now created in Linear, how the ExpenseBot data pipeline work relates to cost data reliability (Dmitri's schema validation on ENG-212, Lena's pipeline hardening on ENG-217, and Anh's type validation on ENG-218 all touch data integrity patterns relevant to the dashboard's ingestion layer), and the overall technical scoping assessment for Samira to review.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Find bill-aws-march-001 from vendor Amazon Web Services (vendor-aws-001) totaling $42,870 with line items: EC2 Compute at $28,450, S3 Storage and Transfer at $5,500, and RDS Database at $8,920. This confirms the actual financial impact in QuickBooks and provides source data for the dashboard's historical trend views component.
Search Linear for existing tickets related to the AWS cost spike or infrastructure monitoring remediation using linear_list_issues (query: "AWS" or "cost alert" or "CloudWatch" or "infrastructure cost" or "budget tag" or "autoscaling" or "cost monitoring"). Discover that no dedicated Linear tickets exist for the AWS remediation items from Samira's task list (cost alert thresholds, budget tags, infrastructure cost estimator, slow query fixes, autoscaling review, monthly cost report). The remediation work was assigned and tracked informally through Slack posts in C009 and C003 but was never formalized into Linear issues. This is a significant finding for the scoping summary: the prompt asks to link the new dashboard issues to existing remediation tickets and to comment on the RCA tracking ticket, but neither exists in Linear as a formal issue. The agent should note this tracking gap.
Search Linear for ExpenseBot project tickets and Anh's engineering work using linear_list_issues (query: "expense" or "dashboard" or "type validation" or project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer") or linear_list_issues (assignee: "moveops_anh_nguyen"). Find Anh's assigned tickets: issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208, Build rule configuration UI for per-client policy management, state: todo, priority P2, assigned to Anh), issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218, Dashboard: Add type validation to policy config editor, state: in_progress, priority P2, assigned to Anh), and issue_bulk_export (ENG-213, Dashboard: Bulk export and policy version history, state: backlog, priority P3, assigned to Anh). Also find related project tickets that touch data integrity: issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214, fix Vectral config, in_progress, assigned to Lena), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215, fix Mosaic config with malformed JSON types, in_progress, assigned to Lena), and issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217, harden policy ingestion pipeline to remove silent fallback and add type enforcement, in_progress, assigned to Lena). These tickets establish what data integrity and pipeline reliability work already exists on the engineering board and whether any of it touches the cost data pipeline the dashboard would rely on.
Search Linear for Dmitri's schema validation and audit work using linear_list_issues (query: "schema" or "validation" or "duplicate" or "audit" or assignee: "moveops_dmitri_volkov"). Find issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216, full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses, state: in_progress, priority: Urgent, assigned to Dmitri), issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212, policy config validation and malformed data handling, state: in_progress, priority P2, assigned to Dmitri), and issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220, stipend duplicate detection using receipt hash fingerprinting and PDF metadata similarity scoring, state: todo, priority P1, assigned to Dmitri). Read comments on these tickets using linear_list_comments. On ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) find comment_dmitri_audit_results documenting the completed audit of 47 expenses: 39 accurate, 5 incorrectly rejected at Vectral, 3 mistakenly approved at Mosaic, resulting in 83% accuracy and $885 total financial exposure. On ENG-212 find comment_dmitri_validation_tests listing six implemented schema validation tests (type assertions, required fields, config-vs-source comparison, exclusion list enum validation, positive limit values, and no silent fallback), all passing with corrected configs but failing with the original production configs. On ENG-220 find comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context noting Dmitri will begin Layer 1 hash dedup implementation and will work with Lena on ingestion pipeline changes, with Lena confirming S3 hash data is already accessible. These tickets and comments show the current state of data pipeline reliability work and whether any of it could affect cost data ingestion for the dashboard.
Search Slack #engineering (C003) for discussions about data validation and pipeline reliability using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "type validation" or "schema validation" or "data validation" or "pipeline" or "ingestion", filter_in_channel: "C003"). Find Anh's message proposing to add type-checking in the dashboard's policy config editor to prevent non-numeric limits and non-array exclusion lists from being saved as JSON, described as a belt-and-suspenders approach, with thumbsup reactions from both Samira and Lena. Find Dmitri's reply agreeing and listing schema validation priorities: type assertions for all config fields, required field checks, generated configs compared against source spreadsheet values, exclusion list entries validated against category enums, and recommending a full audit of all 47 auto-processed expenses. Find Samira's message taking responsibility for the silent fallback in the ingestion pipeline, asking Dmitri to prioritize validation tests and Lena to fix both client configs immediately. Find Lena's earlier message about having implemented basic schema validation in the policy config loader ensuring it rejects JSON missing essential fields, but noting that the effects of subtly malformed data like exclusion lists with wrong types or limits stored as strings instead of numbers had not been tested. These threads establish the data validation concerns and pipeline reliability issues that may be relevant to the cost monitoring dashboard's data sources.
Look up the #engineering Slack channel ID using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel"). Confirm that channel C003 corresponds to #engineering for posting the technical scoping summary. Also note C009 for #root-cause-aws-spike and C005 for #finance from the investigation phase.
Look up the Engineering team in Linear using linear_list_teams or linear_get_team (query: "Engineering"). Find team_engineering with key "ENG" which is needed for creating the new cost monitoring project and issues.
Create a new project in Linear for the cost monitoring dashboard work using linear_create_project (name: "Cost Monitoring Dashboard" or similar, team: "team_engineering", description covering the goal of giving engineering and finance real-time visibility into infrastructure resource consumption to prevent future cost surprises like the March 2026 AWS spike triggered by the Vantage Distributed onboarding).
Create four separate Linear issues under the new cost monitoring project using linear_create_issue for each, all with team: "team_engineering" and project referencing the newly created project ID. Issue 1: Real-time resource consumption tracking, covering a live dashboard view of current AWS compute, storage, and database usage with per-service breakdown. Issue 2: Threshold-based alerting, covering configurable notifications at defined percentages of the monthly baseline, connecting to the P0 CloudWatch plus Slack integration that Lena started but may not have completed. Issue 3: Per-client cost attribution, covering the ability to break down infrastructure costs by customer using the budget tagging approach Lena began implementing via Terraform. Issue 4: Historical trend views, covering time-series visualization of monthly costs for spotting patterns, with the March 2026 spike as the reference event showing costs jumping from approximately $14,300 baseline to $42,870.
Attempt to link the new issues to existing remediation tickets from the AWS incident using linear_update_issue to add related issue references or update descriptions. Since no dedicated AWS remediation Linear tickets exist (discovered in OE 6), use linear_update_issue to embed references to the Slack-based remediation tracking (C009 resolution summary, C003 task list) in the new issue descriptions, and note that remediation items were never formalized into Linear issues. If the agent finds any existing engineering tickets tangentially related to infrastructure monitoring, those could be linked. The absence of formal tracking tickets is itself a finding that should be noted in the scoping summary.
Check the status of the remediation controls that were supposed to be implemented after the AWS spike and flag any that look stale. The agent should cross-reference Samira's P0/P1/P2 task list from C003 (CloudWatch cost alerts, budget tags, infrastructure cost estimator, slow query fixes, autoscaling review, monthly cost report) against what was actually completed. Lena said she would have cost alerts and budget tags ready by Wednesday (around April 2) but there is no follow-up confirmation in Slack or Linear that these were completed. The slow RDS queries were described as a quick fix but no completion was posted. The infrastructure cost estimator and autoscaling review were lower priority and likely not started. Add comments on any relevant tickets using linear_create_comment noting which controls appear incomplete or unverified. If specific ExpenseBot tickets like ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) or ENG-218 (dashboard type validation) show stale statuses that do not reflect recent work discussed in Slack, comment on those as well.
Search for what the prompt refers to as "the original RCA tracking ticket" using linear_list_issues (query: "RCA" or "root cause" or "AWS cost" or "cost spike" or "March AWS"). Since no formal RCA tracking ticket exists in Linear for the AWS cost incident, the agent should note this gap. The root cause investigation was conducted entirely through the #root-cause-aws-spike Slack channel (C009) and email correspondence, with a resolution summary posted in C009 but never converted to a Linear issue. The agent should add a comment using linear_create_comment on the most relevant existing ticket, or if no suitable ticket exists, include the current status of the remediation controls in the scoping summary posted to #engineering. The status update should cover: root cause identified (Vantage Distributed onboarding), no misconfiguration, RCA distributed to leadership, CEO directives issued, CloudWatch cost alerts started by Lena with no confirmed completion, budget tags started with no confirmed completion, pricing model review meeting referenced by Marcus but outcome not tracked in engineering channels, infrastructure cost estimator not yet started, slow query fixes described as quick but not confirmed, and autoscaling review not yet started.
Post the technical scoping summary in the #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: summary content). The summary should cover: what was found during the AWS cost spike investigation (root cause was Vantage Distributed onboarding of 1,200+ users starting March 21, autoscaling from 4 to 18 EC2 instances, 47K document uploads, $42,870 March bill versus $14,300 baseline), the current status of each remediation control from Samira's task list with specific notes on which appear incomplete or unverified, the finding that AWS remediation was tracked only through Slack posts with no Linear tickets, the new Cost Monitoring Dashboard project and its four component issues now created in Linear, how the ExpenseBot data pipeline work relates to cost data reliability (Dmitri's schema validation on ENG-212, Lena's pipeline hardening on ENG-217, and Anh's type validation on ENG-218 all touch data integrity patterns relevant to the dashboard's ingestion layer), and the overall technical scoping assessment for Samira to review.
